title: Fall is Blow-Out Season: Tips for Winterizing Your Sprinkler System (Part 1/4)
content:
Fall is Blow-Out Season: Tips for Winterizing Your Sprinkler System

As colder weather steadily creeps in and fall fades to winter, it’s time to start putting things to bed for the season. We call this “blow-out season” because if any water freezes in your sprinkler pipes, it will expand, which can cause them to break or burst.

This is why it’s important to “blow out” any remaining water before things get too cold and start freezing. Smart maintenance during the fall can help prevent costly repairs down the road. So, if you want to prevent winter blowouts, follow these tips to winterize your sprinkler system this fall.

Winterizing Your Sprinkler System

Can you winterize your own sprinkler system? Yes, you can if you have access to some specialty equipment. That said, it’s important to be cautious and follow all instructions carefully to avoid hurting yourself or damaging your sprinkler system.

Manual Procedure

If you have a manual drain valve or drain cap, be sure to wear eye protection before completing this task, as these systems are pressurized and could cause injury if opened before the pressure is relieved.

Turn off your sprinkler system’s mainline shutoff valve.

Open one of the control valves (manually or electronically from the timer) to relieve pressure on the mainline.

SLOWLY open the manual drain valve.

Repeat this procedure for ALL manual drain valves on your sprinkler system mainline.

Automatic System Procedure

The automatic drain valve is a spring-loaded device that is installed on the sprinkler heads or pipes. Automatic drain valves will remove water from the system every time it is shut off. As such, you should not need to manually intervene. Still, it’s important to check for excessive puddling on the surface of the soil, which could indicate one of the drains has become stuck open.

Blow-Out the Lines

Make sure you wear eye protection during this process and be careful to avoid creating excess pressure, which could damage your system and cause injury from flying debris. DO NOT stand over any irrigation components during air blowout. The air pressure should not exceed 50 pounds per square inch (psi).

For best results, it is recommended that you use a large compressor (50-70 gallons) that can keep a continual flow of pressure of 185 cfm or greater. The compressor should be regulated to provide 75-85 psi, depending on how big the lines or zones are in your system.

If you do not have the right equipment for this procedure and are worried about doing something wrong, you should contact a professional to provide the service for you.

If you do intend to take care of the blowout yourself, make sure you OPEN ONE VALVE BEFORE STARTING THE AIR COMPRESSOR, as this will help balance the pressure, so you don’t damage the system or blow any head apart.

title: Fall is Blow-Out Season: Tips for Winterizing Your Sprinkler System (Part 2/4)
content:
should contact a professional to provide the service for you. If you do intend to take care of the blowout yourself, make sure you OPEN ONE VALVE BEFORE STARTING THE AIR COMPRESSOR, as this will help balance the pressure, so you don’t damage the system or blow any head apart.

Additionally, keep in mind that this process will create heat in the lines, which needs to be regulated so as not to warp any of the pipes or fittings. You DO NOT need to blow every single drop of water out. Make sure you don’t run the air too long with gear drive rotors, as it can heat the rotor wiper seal and melt the rotor head together, which will require costly repairs come spring.

Blow-out procedure when activating valves from a controller:

Close the mainline sprinkler shutoff valve.

Relieve the water pressure on the mainline by activating a circuit or zone from your controller. Make sure you activate the circuit that is furthest from the air connection before blowing air into the piping.

Attach the air compressor hose to a blow-out adapter.

Set the pressure regulating valve on the compressor to 50 psi.

Turn the compressor on and gradually increase the airflow until the sprinkler heads pop up. The amount of airflow or volume needed will depend on the length of your pipes and the number of heads.

DO NOT blow any circuit for more than 2 minutes at a time, as heat from the compressed air can damage the pipes and other components.

Switch to another station or zone by advancing the timer to the next circuit. DO NOT turn the controller off at any time during this process until the compressor is shut off.

To ensure adequate drainage, repeat this cycle two or more times, activating each one from the timer until nothing more than a fine mist appears from the heads.

After you have blown out all zones, leave one zone on while you shut off the compressor.

Unhook the air compressor from the adapter.

Turn the timer to “OFF”.

Blowout procedure when activating valves manually:

This process is only to be used if your system doesn’t have electric valves connected to a controller.

Close the main shutoff valve.

Slowly open the manual shutoff valve handle on one of your zone control valves to relieve water pressure on the mainline.

Attach the air compressor hose to a blow-out adapter.

Set the pressure regulating valve on the compressor to 50 psi.

Turn on the irrigation zone you want to blow out.

Turn the air compressor on and gradually increase the flow of air until the sprinkler heads pop up. Again, the amount of airflow and volume needed will depend on how long your pipes run and how many heads there are.

DO NOT blow any zone for more than 2 minutes, as the heat created by the air compressor can damage components.

title: Fall is Blow-Out Season: Tips for Winterizing Your Sprinkler System (Part 3/4)
content:
air until the sprinkler heads pop up. Again, the amount of airflow and volume needed will depend on how long your pipes run and how many heads there are. DO NOT blow any zone for more than 2 minutes, as the heat created by the air compressor can damage components.

After 2 minutes, turn the compressor off and allow all the air to completely purge from the compressor tank and sprinkler system.

Turn on the next irrigation control valve you want to blow out.

Turn off the last control valve you just blew out.

Repeat this process until you have completed two or more blowout cycles per zone. If the process was successful, you should only see a fine mist blowing from each head.

Turn the air compressor off and allow any air left in the storage tank or irrigation components to disperse before touching the air hose or valves.

Unhook the compressor from the adapter.

Additional Winterizing Tips

After your system is fully drained, you’ll want to take a few extra steps to fully winterize your system. This includes shutting off the water source and insulating your valve boxes and backflow preventers. Any other steps you may need to take will depend on your system. If you aren’t sure what to do, it’s best to contact a professional to handle winter maintenance for you.

A smart WiFi-based timer, like the Rain Bird ARC Series Controller , can help as it provides numerous features that can help you more easily maintain your sprinkler system, such as:

Automatic seasonal adjust to match the season, weather, and temperature

Notifications and alerts for freeze warnings

Easy control of all zones (or stations)

Rain/freeze sensor, master valve, and pump start relay circuits

Professional Sprinkler Winterization

Though you can winterize and blow out your sprinkler system on your own, it is a tedious process and should be handled with care. If you have any concerns about handling this process yourself, it is advised that you contact a professional to take care of your winterization maintenance for you.

Though having a professional handle this task for you might seem like an added expense, it can save you time and money in the long run. If you don’t perform winter maintenance adequately yourself, it can damage your system, which will require costly repairs come spring. So, having a professional handle the maintenance for you can end up saving you from more expensive repairs down the road.

Whether you hire a professional or handle it yourself, winterization of your sprinkler system is an essential step that should not be overlooked. Seasonal maintenance can go a long way toward extending the life of your system and protecting it from damage, so everything is ready when spring arrives.

title: 3 Practical Tips to Enhance Your Landscape Watering Efficiency (Part 1/2)
content:
3 Practical Tips to Enhance Your Landscape Watering Efficiency

With increasing water scarcity and changing climate, maintaining your landscape is more important than ever. Some states require homeowners to reduce outdoor watering to preserve water resources. Even in areas where restrictions aren't enforced, responsible water use is crucial for the environment, communities and our own pockets. Water conservation is a critical issue that demands our attention. As pioneers of efficient watering, Rain Bird believes in striking a balance between maintaining lush, green landscapes and preserving water resources.

In this article, we'll discuss the relevance of automatic lawn irrigation for sustainable irrigation solutions and offer tips to help drought-proof your yard.

The Importance of Lawn Irrigation

A well-maintained yard not only adds curb appeal to your home but also provides several environmental, social, and economic benefits. Your lawn is a natural air filter that reduces heat and absorbs pollutants. A well-irrigated landscape also promotes biodiversity by providing habitat for insects, birds, and other wildlife. Moreover, studies have shown that well-maintained landscapes can increase property value by up to 20%.

It often goes overlooked that without watering the lawn, trees don’t get the water they need. Trees provide much more than shade, they help clean the air by producing oxygen and absorbing carbon dioxide. They also provide homes for birds and other wildlife. When trees are deprived of water, they become stressed which makes them susceptible to disease and pests.

Tips for Smart Watering

Automatic lawn irrigation systems ensure your lawn gets the right amount of water at the right time. They also help conserve water by preventing overwatering, leading to healthier plants and significant water savings. This makes an automatic sprinkler system a smart choice for sustainable landscaping. Here are some tips to help you make the most of your irrigation system:

1. Water Early

Consider the time of day and frequency of your watering. Early morning is the most efficient time to water your lawn, as lower temperatures mean less water is lost to evaporation. Adjust your watering schedule based on weather conditions to water less frequently during rainy periods. The Rain Bird ARC 8 Smart Residential Controller lets you program your irrigation settings from your smartphone and connects to online weather services to adjust its schedule to your local forecast.

2. Use “Cycle and Soak” to Avoid Run-Off

Some California jurisdictions mandate limited watering days. Watering less frequently can promote deep root growth, which enables plants to withstand periods without water. However, watering for too long in one period can cause wasteful run-off. Instead, use “cycle and soak” scheduling to set up multiple irrigation cycles, followed by a “soak” period to provide sufficient water to plants without causing run-off.

3. Monitor your Water Bill and Check for Leaks

If your water bill seems high, or has increased without an obvious reason, check your irrigation system for leaks and clean, fix or replace any equipment that is not working. If need be, contact a professional irrigation contractor in your area.

title: 3 Practical Tips to Enhance Your Landscape Watering Efficiency (Part 2/2)
content:
3. Monitor your Water Bill and Check for Leaks If your water bill seems high, or has increased without an obvious reason, check your irrigation system for leaks and clean, fix or replace any equipment that is not working. If need be, contact a professional irrigation contractor in your area.

Upgrade Your System to More Efficient Equipment

Studies have shown that installing updated, more efficient irrigation technology conserves more water than restricting consumer use. Some states and communities offer rebates to support irrigation equipment upgrades. Here are the easiest ways to make your existing irrigation system more efficient:

Weather-Based Controllers

Weather-based controllers, such as the Rain Bird ARC Series Smart Residential Controller , can save an average home nearly 7,600 gallons of water annually by using local weather data to adjust irrigation to prevent over-watering.

Pressure Regulating Stems (PRS)

For every 10 psi in reduced water pressure, there is a 5% increase in water savings for sprays and a 7% increase in water savings for rotors. California has mandated all new sprays sold in the state have PRS technology because of the proven efficiency increase. You can upgrade your existing sprays using this now-mandated technology.

Rotary Nozzles for Sprays

Rotary nozzles retrofitted into existing spray bodies can improve water savings by up to 30% compared to standard spray nozzles. Rotary nozzles apply water more slowly and accurately than standard spray nozzles, reducing run-off and erosion and allowing the soil time to absorb more water.

Drip Irrigation

Low-volume drip irrigation systems maintain healthy landscapes with over 90% efficiency by delivering water at or near each plant’s root zone. The slow, targeted application of water reduces run-off and evaporation. Drip irrigation is especially convenient for container plantings and small areas like flower beds or around trees and shrubs.

The importance of water conservation cannot be overstated. As climate changes continue to intensify, implementing efficient watering systems like automatic lawn irrigation becomes an obvious choice. Tools such as weather-based controllers , pressure regulating stems, rotary nozzles, and drip irrigation systems can drastically reduce water usage while ensuring your landscape remains verdant and healthy. By making these smart, sustainable choices, we can all play our part in preserving our planet's precious water resources. Remember, every drop saved today contributes to a more sustainable tomorrow.

title: Protect Your Home from Wildfires (Part 1/2)
content:
Create a Green Barrier to Protect Your Home from Wildfires

As we continue to experience unpredictable and extreme weather patterns, the threat of wildfires becomes ever more present, particularly for those who live in vulnerable regions. According to CalFire, wildfires burned 1,077,711 acres and damaged or destroyed more than 2,481 structures in 2024 in California alone. In this article, we explore the role of lawn sprinklers and other simple methods to help protect your home from wildfires.

Establish Green Space

The green spaces in your lawn and property can provide a buffer from wildfires. Maintaining a well-irrigated, lush green lawn can act as a natural fire barrier because green vegetation has a higher flash point and is harder for fires to burn through, given its high moisture content, which slows approaching flames.

Establishing such a vibrant, green perimeter around your home not only enhances its aesthetic appeal but also creates a defensible space. This space, ideally spanning at least 30 feet from the house, reduces a wildfire's ability to transfer heat or flames to your property.

And there's more to green spaces than just lawns. Consider incorporating fire-resistant plants into your garden. These plants retain a high level of moisture, resist ignition, and if they do catch fire, they burn at a lower intensity. Some examples include French lavender, red monkey flower, California fuchsia, and the common yarrow.

Clear Brush

Of course, it's not just about having a green space, but keeping it maintained. Regularly clearing out dead plants, leaves, and tree branches goes a long way in preventing these potential fire fuels from inviting a wildfire to your doorstep.

In essence, a well-nurtured, green space around your home plays a critical role in your wildfire defense strategy. It's a beautiful, living barrier that uses nature's own resources, water and green vegetation, to shield your home from the threat of wildfires.

The Role of Brass Impact Sprinklers

When it comes to the fight against wildfires, the U.S. Forest Service has relied on a specific tool : the Brass Impact Sprinkler. So, what makes them such an effective tool for wildfire prevention? The answer lies in their design and function.

Brass impact sprinklers operate by projecting water in a circular pattern, covering a large area. These sprinklers are designed to mimic the effect of a moderate rain shower, saturating the ground and any potential fire fuels such as grass, leaves, and brush. Thanks to their brass construction, these sprinklers are robust and resistant to the heat of a fire that would otherwise melt plastic. They serve as a first line of defense, creating a ring of moisture around your property that can deter approaching wildfires.

title: Protect Your Home from Wildfires (Part 2/2)
content:
fuels such as grass, leaves, and brush. Thanks to their brass construction, these sprinklers are robust and resistant to the heat of a fire that would otherwise melt plastic. They serve as a first line of defense, creating a ring of moisture around your property that can deter approaching wildfires.

These sprinklers are installed above ground and can be easily adjusted to control the direction and distance of the water, allowing you to effectively target the areas most at risk. When connected to a reliable water source, they can maintain this defensive ring of moisture for extended periods, an essential feature during prolonged periods of high fire risk.

By incorporating these sprinklers into your green defense strategy, you are taking a proactive step towards protecting your home, mirroring the preventative measures used by professionals in the field.

Setting Up Fire Barriers on Your Property

When it comes to fortifying your home against wildfires, establishing fire barriers is instrumental. Here are a few tips to help you set up effective fire barriers.

Identify Vulnerable Areas: Start by identifying areas on your property most vulnerable to wildfires. These could be regions with dry vegetation, wooden structures, or spots that are closest to wildland areas. Prioritize these areas when setting up your fire barriers.

Use Fire-Resistant Materials: If you're constructing physical barriers like fences or walls, consider using fire-resistant materials like stone, metal, or treated wood. These materials can withstand high temperatures and slow a fire's progress.

Plant Fire-Resistant Greenery: Incorporating fire-resistant plants into your landscaping can act as a natural barrier. These plants, such as succulents or certain species of shrubs and trees, resist igniting because of their high moisture content.

Strategically Place Lawn Sprinklers: Position your sprinklers, particularly the robust Brass Impact Sprinklers, to cover as much of your at-risk areas as possible. They can create a protective moisture ring around your property, making it difficult for wildfires to advance.

Maintain Your Fire Barriers: Regularly trim and maintain your plants, repair fences or walls, and ensure your sprinklers are in good working condition. A fire barrier is only as effective as its upkeep.

Ensure Accessibility: Make sure your property remains accessible to firefighters. Fire barriers should not block access routes; instead, they should guide fire crews around your property and protect evacuation paths.

Remember, setting up fire barriers is a proactive measure – a way to prepare a safer future for your home and community. Fire barriers work hand in hand with maintaining green spaces and the intelligent use of water to create a comprehensive wildfire defense plan.

title: Tree Irrigation Solutions (Part 1/2)
content:
How to Give Your Trees a Lifetime of Health and Beauty

Is your home surrounded by healthy, thriving trees? A yard full of beautiful trees adds curb appeal and provides a sense of natural tranquility. Trees also serve practical purposes like conserving water and improving energy efficiency with shade from the hot summer sun.

Let’s look at how Rain Bird can help you maintain healthy trees in your yard.

Match Your Trees to Your Yard

Every yard needs the right trees for its location. Some trees thrive in hot climates and others flourish in colder zones.

Where is your home located on the USDA plant hardiness map ? In warmer regions, including the U.S. South and Southwest zones 8 to 10, trees like Cypress and Magnolias flourish. Cooler northern regions are home to hardier trees like American Sycamores and Red Maples.

Select the right trees for your zone while keeping your unique yard in mind. Weather and rainfall impact which trees will be the right fit. For example, the trees in a desert community will receive ample sunshine and minimal water, so they’ll need effective water management with smart monitors and adjustable rain valves

Tree spacing and proper sunlight exposure encourage root growth, allowing trees to absorb nutrients and water efficiently. Some tree species prosper in full sun, while others prefer a break from sunlight in partial shade.

Learn More About Your Soil Type

Under your grass and trees, the soil provides nourishment and stability. Every yard has a soil type or even multiple soil types.

Healthy soil offers a mix of organic matter, air pockets, minerals, and tiny living microbes. Soil has a pH level ranging from acidic to alkaline, which you can find easily using a home soil testing kit. While most plants thrive in pH-neutral soil, trees grow well in a broad range of pH levels. You have plenty of options.

The health of your yard matters to your trees. The turf should be well-draining yet able to retain enough moisture to meet the tree's water needs. Think of it as creating a living ecosystem to nourish your grass, plants, trees, and your enjoyment of your yard.

Tips for Nourishing Your Yard with Irrigation

Provide deep, balanced watering

More water doesn't always translate to healthier trees. Overwatering deprives the tree roots of oxygen, leading to root rot and a weak tree. On the other hand, too little water can cause the tree to dry out and struggle.

The key is to strike the right balance. We recommend deep watering once a week for most trees. Water application should match the tree’s needs and environment, accounting for humidity, temperature fluctuation, light exposure, wind, and season.

Plan for easy irrigation

Start early for years of easy, stress-free watering. When you first plant young trees, set the stage for healthy growth with an efficient and cost-effective root watering system . These systems minimize runoff and evaporation while ensuring deep and vibrant root growth.

Enjoy years of flexibility

title: Tree Irrigation Solutions (Part 2/2)
content:
Plan for easy irrigation Start early for years of easy, stress-free watering. When you first plant young trees, set the stage for healthy growth with an efficient and cost-effective root watering system . These systems minimize runoff and evaporation while ensuring deep and vibrant root growth. Enjoy years of flexibility

Flexible solutions grow with your trees. A square nozzle sprinkler is an ideal choice for maturing trees because it wets a large area and adjusts as your tree ages. If you have dry and sandy soil, you may need to supplement the natural rainfall with additional water.

Drip irrigation adds water slowly and efficiently, applying it directly to the roots around the tree without wasting water elsewhere. A subsurface dripline is a versatile and puddle-free tree irrigation solution for long-term peace of mind.

As you can see, tree care is easy when you have the right tools and techniques. A bit of extra planning will help your trees thrive for a lifetime.

title: Top 3 Best Ways to Water Trees (Part 1/3)
content:
The Three Best Ways to Water Your Trees

Trees cleanse the air and give your home a break from the sun. They add charm, natural beauty, and dappled shade that’s ideal for turf grass and landscaping plants.

What does it take to water the trees in your yard? Here are our top tips for keeping your trees hydrated and thriving for a lifetime .

Plant With a Watering Plan

A proactive watering plan ensures each tree receives just the right amount of water from seedling to maturity. Before you plant new trees or add new irrigation features, map out a basic tree plan.

It doesn’t have to be complicated. Simply draw outlines of your house and yard, then note each tree’s location. Ensure each tree will have plenty of growth space based on its full, mature size. If you need extra help, consider working with an experienced arborist, tree nursery or irrigation contractor to develop a custom plan for your property.

Consider your trees’ needs . Saplings and young trees generally require more frequent watering than mature trees because their roots are not yet deeply established. Beware of overwatering, which can drown the young roots with too much fluid.

Mature trees need less frequent but deeper watering at regular intervals. Deep watering is particularly important during drought, which places more stress than usual on your trees.

As time goes by, use your tree map to decide on new tree locations and the right watering features, like sprinklers and drip lines. Below, we’ll explain more about the watering features you can use to provide ideal hydration.

Nourish From the Roots

Although you might envision watering your trees from the ground level, root watering is the secret to decades of healthy trees. Also known as subsurface root irrigation, a root watering system directs the flow of water directly at the tree roots rather than applying it from above the land's surface.

This encourages roots to grow steadily away from the root ball, rather than remaining stunted or reaching toward the ground’s surface. As a result of root watering, the tree becomes more deeply nestled in the ground. It will not only drink water more efficiently throughout its life but also root firmly enough to resist windstorms and other damage.

Benefits of Root Watering

Consistency. Root watering provides up to 95% uniformity in water application.

Aesthetics. Below-surface watering and bubbler systems are almost invisible to the eye.

Health. A University of Arizona study found trees grew stronger and roots grew thicker with the increased oxygen and nutrients from root watering.

Water Your Trees Multiple Ways

One of the best ways to enhance watering efficiency is to add multiple watering options for your trees and plants. In some areas, drip irrigation might be the best solution. Other areas may call for sprinklers or subsurface irrigation.

1. Drip Irrigation

title: Top 3 Best Ways to Water Trees (Part 2/3)
content:
from root watering. Water Your Trees Multiple Ways One of the best ways to enhance watering efficiency is to add multiple watering options for your trees and plants. In some areas, drip irrigation might be the best solution. Other areas may call for sprinklers or subsurface irrigation. 1. Drip Irrigation

Drip irrigators apply water in the root zone. This spot watering method is perfect for areas like decorative beds with a mix of flowers, bushes, and trees. Water slowly drips its nutrients into your tree root areas on a schedule you can program for maximum effectiveness.

Bubblers and micro sprayers are also types of drip irrigation you can use to direct water flow to specific areas. These localized, low-spray options allow precise targeting of water to prevent overspray and ensure each plant or tree receives optimal water.

2. Sprinklers

Did you know sprinklers are math marvels for tree-filled lawns? They use built-in technology with adjustable calculations to deliver just the right amount of water to each tree.

A low-volume square nozzle sprinkler has many potential configurations and fits many types of spray heads and watering features. Use these sprinklers to create a grid and ensure you’re watering the entire root base around every tree, preventing uneven growth.

An automated sprinkler system prevents water waste, overwatering, and underwatering. Timed water application also works conveniently with your schedule, allowing you to water only when you’re not out using your yard.

3. Subsurface Drip Lines

A subsurface drip line is perhaps the most efficient and tree-friendly watering system you can add to your yard. After its installation, it’s an almost maintenance-free irrigation solution. Your trees stay free of puddling and oversaturation, enjoying a stable and consistent flow of nourishing water.

Some homeowners prefer subsurface irrigation for design and aesthetic reasons. You can create a buried watering system that won’t interfere with the beautiful, landscaped look of your yard. Subsurface systems come in a nearly endless variety of layouts and configurations to suit your trees perfectly.

The Root Booster series of products from Rain Bird offer a root watering solution that's easy to install for a variety of solutions. These innovative products eliminate the need to rely solely on rain or automatic irrigation systems. Root Booster's non-woven material actively captures water that might otherwise dissipate through evaporation or drainage, making it accessible to the plant's roots whenever needed, effectively extending the intervals between watering sessions, saving you time, water, and money.

Bonus Tip: Tree Stories

As you can see, watering your trees is much easier when you have the right irrigation features. A thoughtful watering plan is the secret to decades of healthy growth.

title: Make Sense of WaterSense to Help Your Wallet and the Planet (Part 1/2)
content:
Make Sense of WaterSense to Help Your Wallet and the Planet

Proper watering saves money, time, and precious natural resources. Did you know a faulty sprinkler can waste 25,000 gallons of water and add $280 to your water bill every season?

These figures come from the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) WaterSense program . For more than 15 years, the program has been winning the battle against water waste.

Here’s what you need to know about setting up your home irrigation system to benefit from good WaterSense .

What Is the WaterSense Program?

WaterSense was established in 2006 to meet guidelines in the U.S. Energy Policy and Conservation Act of 1992. EPA WaterSense certification means a product is designed to use at least 20% less water while reducing energy consumption.

When a company’s products have WaterSense certification, they save water and energy through superior design and performance. The EPA requires WaterSense products to “perform as well as or better than regular models.”

Across the U.S., WaterSense is making an enormously positive impact. It saves more than 1 trillion gallons of water every year that would otherwise be wasted.

Since 2006, WaterSense has helped U.S. homeowners save more than $170 billion on their home energy and water bills. The EPA estimates that the program has preserved 880 billion kilowatt-hours of electricity, adding to the global mission of energy savings.

Money-Saving Rebates From WaterSense

The WaterSense program doesn’t directly provide discounts for homeowners who want to preserve water. Instead, it alerts you to rebate partners offering money back on water-efficient products.

Most WaterSense rebate partners are cities and districts that offer savings on water-efficient purchases. For example, if you live in Southern California , you can get a $80 rebate on a smart controller that costs under $100 , reducing the price of your upgrade to just $20. Other areas may have similar rebates. Some retailers also have resources to help you find and apply for rebates. Both The Home Depot and Lowe’s have rebate centers.

The main types of products you can buy with WaterSense rebates are bathroom faucets, irrigation services, showerheads, soil moisture sensors, sprinkler bodies, tank and flushometer toilets, urinals, and weather-based irrigation controllers.

If you live in California, you’ll be happy to learn that 47 districts offer WaterSense rebates on a wide variety of home and business products. California is a nationwide leader in water preservation with a consistently high level of participation in the program.

Other areas that have received EPA honors for using WaterSense rebates include major cities in Georgia, Florida, Virginia, and Texas. Most—but not all—states include at least a few participating cities.

Below, you can learn more about how to identify WaterSense products, including highly water-efficient Rain Bird timers and sprinklers. Click here to view available rebates.

Look for the WaterSense Label

Here’s something important to know about WaterSense. More than 900 water control products sold in the U.S. meet the program’s standards, but participation in WaterSense is voluntary . Thousands more products don’t qualify.

Believe it or not, many types of yard watering products aren’t purpose-built for water preservation. Instead, they may focus on affordability or a type of custom irrigation that doesn’t align with the WaterSense vision of a sustainable future.

To select the best water-saving products, look for the WaterSense label showing the product’s EPA-approved status. You can also use the WaterSense product search online to check on a product’s water efficiency before you make a purchase.

Rain Bird WaterSense Options for Your Yard

Rain Bird is dedicated to making it easier to nourish your yard while saving water. Our WaterSense products include smart timers and lawn sprinklers , rotary and pressure-regulated stems (PRS) with exceptional performance and guaranteed efficient water use.

Smart Irrigation Controllers

The ARC Series irrigation controllers from Rain Bird, are a simple approach to smart watering. These app-based watering timers help the average household save up to 30% more water, which exceeds the WaterSense baseline of 20% savings.

They’re not only sensible but also fun to use. Adjust your irrigation settings from your mobile device anytime from anywhere. Set a schedule for perfect watering without waste. You can also allow the controller to adjust automatically based on local weather forecasts and previous averages.

Pressure Regulated Sprays

Rain Bird’s 1800 Series Pressure Regulated Spray Heads provide a wide range of spraying options so you can select just the right pattern and pressure for your needs. The sprayers in this series are WaterSense certified with pressure regulators to enhance efficiency . These PRS nozzles maintain constant outlet pressure at 30 psi for the most efficient performance, minimizing unwanted misting and increasing watering uniformity across zones for greener results with less water. Imagine a lifetime of savings with an ultra-efficient sprayer like this.

Keep the WaterSense program in mind as you select the products you use to water your trees , bushes, flowers, and grass. As you can see, WaterSense just makes good sense.

title: Water-Smart Gardening: Innovative Strategies for Lawn Irrigation (Part 1/3)
content:
Water-Smart Gardening: Innovative Strategies for Lawn Irrigation

Smart watering is becoming easier than ever. New smart watering features connect with home monitoring systems, making it simple to save water, energy, time, and money.

Do you have a smart home system in place? Let’s look at how you could use it to nourish your lawn and garden with just the right amount of moisture.

Watering with Smart Home Systems

A smart watering system integrates multiple watering activities to give you better control. It allows you to easily adjust your sprinklers, misters, drip lines, water pressure, spray patterns, and other types of watering on your property.

Many of these systems can also track the weather to adapt automatically to previous precipitation averages. When you have a series of rainy days, they limit watering. During a drought, they provide extra moisture to protect your plants.

Smart watering systems naturally conserve water because they do all the tracking for you. Watering features can hook into your property’s overall smart home system for optimal control.

Imagine adjusting your sprinklers by saying, “Hey, Alexa.” Enjoy saving hundreds of dollars on your water bill by doing nothing—because your smart watering system is doing it all for you.

The Best Ways to Water Smart

Many people are unaware of innovative watering methods simply because they’ve never tried them. Here’s a quick overview of tips to make smart watering easier.

Drip Irrigation

Drip irrigation uses tubing and emitters to slowly distribute water to the right spots at the right times. Drip tubing can be placed at ground level or buried underground.

In this watering system, small emitters called drippers allow water to flow from the main distribution tubing in a custom arrangement for your landscaping. With the drip tubing and emitters placed close to individual plants, water soaks directly to the roots minimizing moisture loss from evaporation and runoff. This is an excellent way to maintain a vegetable garden or newly planted trees.

According to water absorption studies, drip irrigation can effectively cover 25% more area than the average sprinkler system, while a well-designed drip system typically delivers more than 95% water efficiency.

Cycle and Soak Method

One of the smartest ways to water your lawn without waste is through the time-tested cycle and soak method. This technique releases water in multiple shorter cycles allowing ample time to soak into the ground.

The on-and-off soaking rhythm allows plants the time they need to absorb water fully. Plants and tree roots can’t take on too much water at once or they’ll become waterlogged and resist absorption.

Use the cycle and soak method to keep your yard healthy and vibrant. Generally, a 60-minute soaking cycle is best for the average home. This means the grass and plants have a full hour to absorb water before another timed soak begins.

Smart Timers

Smart timers make it easy to water successfully, even if you know little about irrigation.

title: Water-Smart Gardening: Innovative Strategies for Lawn Irrigation (Part 2/3)
content:
yard healthy and vibrant. Generally, a 60-minute soaking cycle is best for the average home. This means the grass and plants have a full hour to absorb water before another timed soak begins. Smart Timers Smart timers make it easy to water successfully, even if you know little about irrigation.

Rain Bird ARC Series smart irrigation controllers are a simple way to get started with smart home watering. They connect through your home’s WiFi, so you can control them from your mobile device. You can even speak to them through Amazon Alexa or the Google Assistant.

To program the controller, select one of three programs to customize based on the conditions for your yard. From there, you can set it to make automatic adjustments according to soil type, seasonal weather averages or forecasted rain.

For example, with rising temperatures, your landscape may require more water. The problem with watering in long durations is you may start to get runoff or pooling if you have heavy soils or a sloped zone that is easily oversaturated. Knowing this, your controller can automatically break the watering up into multiple “cycles” with a period of “soak” in between. Moisture deep in the soil promotes deeper roots, which contributes to a thicker, healthier, greener lawn.

Or, when incremental weather is forecasted, this controller can use highly accurate, internet-sourced local weather data based on the zip code input during set-up to automatically delay watering without a separate rain sensor.

Rain Bird has found that these controllers reduce typical water usage by up to 30%. They can also help homeowners meet local watering restrictions, which occur often in water-sensitive areas like California and the desert southwest. Click here to view available rebates.

Making a Difference with Smart Watering

Smart watering makes a positive impact that’s especially noticeable in the long term. You could save thousands of gallons of water per year, lightening the load on the environment and lowering your water bill.

The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) reports that the typical U.S. household uses 70% of its water for indoor household needs and 30% for outdoor yard watering. But in arid zones, outdoor watering can be as much as 60% of total water use.

Without smart features in place, as much as half of all outdoor watering is lost to evaporation, runoff, and wind. You can use smart watering products, including those on the EPA’s WaterSense-certified list, to noticeably limit water loss.

Well-designed irrigation controllers can save as much as $500 and 15,000 gallons of water per household per year. Every drop matters.

If you’re ready to upgrade your home to be more water-smart, take a look at Rain Bird’s online store with timers, controllers, drip irrigators, and sprayers. We aim to take the stress out of watering so you can enjoy your yard every season.

title: Upgrade to a Rain Bird Smart Controller (Part 1/3)
content:
Upgrade Your Controller and Your Lawn

Automated sprinklers have come a long way. Today’s automated watering systems now provide homeowners with more control than ever, largely thanks to smart home systems that can be used to control irrigation.

Smart watering ensures that your lawn gets the irrigation it needs without waste, thanks to smart controllers managed with the same mobile device you use to check the weather, get the scores from the latest game, or order a pizza.

With the new advances in smart irrigation controllers, you might be surprised to learn that your current controller is no longer the latest and greatest. Here are a few reasons why now may be the perfect time to upgrade your controller.

1. Your old controller isn’t as dependable as it used to be

You leave your house every morning at 8:00, and as you back your car out of the driveway, you’re accustomed to seeing the sprinklers leap to life.

But the last few mornings, something’s different. Sometimes, the sprinklers aren’t going, and your lawn is starting to look a little thirsty. Maybe it’s time to upgrade your controller.

As the weather changes, so do the water requirements of your lawn and garden. Reducing the amount of time your system waters during the cool seasons and increasing it in the hotter months creates a healthier landscape and can help you save countless gallons of water. With Rain Bird controllers, you can quickly and easily make a change to increase or decrease the zone watering times by a certain percentage without having to reprogram each zone individually.

2. Share access to your irrigation system

You want to set it and forget it, but sometimes you need to change up your watering plan. Maybe you’ve updated your landscaping and want to modify the amount of water in a certain area or reduce the water usage for a season.

However, when you’re faced with the controller in question, you find yourself wondering which button you need to push to get the results you want. Older irrigation controllers may not be all that intuitive when programming and can leave you frustrated.

Rain Bird’s ARC series irrigation controllers are intuitively designed with all scheduling done at your fingertips with the Rain Bird mobile app while at home or on the road. Just set the watering frequency on odd or even, or certain days of the month, or create a custom watering schedule to occur on specific days of the week. You can even set a cyclic irrigation schedule to occur at specific intervals. Next, simply add start times and watering durations for each zone. With the Rain Bird mobile app, setting up schedules and making changes on the go is a breeze. You can even control your sprinkler system with your voice if you connect it to Amazon Alexa or Google Assistant.

3. Control your irrigation even when you’re not at home

It’s easy to keep tabs on your landscape irrigation events while away from home. With ARC series controllers and the Rain Bird mobile app, you can share access to your irrigation system for a specified time with family members or landscaping and maintenance professionals who may need to adjust programming or make system repairs.

4. Your old controller doesn’t have the flexibility you need

The last thing you want is for whole swaths of your yard to be neglected simply because your controller doesn’t have enough scheduling options or stations to cover all your landscaping. Modern controllers offer greater flexibility in your irrigation with more zones and watering schedules. If your current controller is a 4-station model, but you have areas of your landscaping that are untouched by your sprinkler system, it’s probably time for a 6- or 8-station model. Both the ARC and TRU series controllers have larger station models available.

Older controllers might not have the ability to schedule as many start times, requiring you to water manually at some point in the day. Newer controller models offer up to three individual programs with four start times per program, for a total of 12 start times, providing the ultimate watering flexibility. Imagine the ability to add a cycle and soak schedule based on your unique landscape terrain or soil type. And advanced features like notifications, custom program cycles, and watering delay allow users the ability to accommodate a wide variety of landscape applications while saving time and money.

5. Old controllers don’t have the water-saving features you want

When your sprinkler system fires up in the middle of a torrential downpour, that’s not just a waste of valuable water resources, it’s also a waste of money. Older controller models aren’t equipped to automatically self-adjust based on current weather conditions.

Rain Bird’s ARC series controllers can adjust or even skip daily watering based on your local weather forecast. Additionally, they can be programmed to meet your local watering restrictions, ensuring you’re not breaking the law to keep your lawn looking healthy. Best of all, they are EPA WaterSense certified , acting like a thermostat for your sprinkler system telling your sprinkler system when to turn on and off, using local weather and landscape conditions to tailor watering schedules to actual conditions on the site.

Ready to upgrade? Rain Bird can help!

If you are tired of battling your irrigation controller to get the results you want, maybe it’s time to consider upgrading to a newer model. The latest technology in automatic sprinkler system controllers allows you to test your sprinklers from the lawn, easily update your irrigation schedule, and automatically conserve water.

title: Cultivating Community: Exploring the Beauty of Public Gardens (Part 1/2)
content:
Cultivating Community: Exploring the Beauty of Public Gardens

Nicholas Conservatory & Gardens, Rockford, IL

Public gardens ensure everyone can get on nature’s good side. Did you know Rain Bird ® helped inspire National Public Gardens Day™? We’re delighted to play a role in helping people enjoy the natural world.

Rain Bird worked with the American Public Gardens Association to raise awareness of gardening and water conservation initiatives. In 2010, Congress officially recognized the holiday on the U.S. Senate floor and began a new era of public garden appreciation.

National Public Gardens Day is celebrated each year on the Friday before Mother’s Day. It is an opportunity to visit a public garden or participate in improving the gardens in your community.

What Is a Public Garden?

Just as the name implies, public gardens are open to everyone and welcome curious people to view flowers, trees, and plants they may have never seen before. These gardens contribute to more appreciation, research, and conservation of our world’s most fascinating flora.

The American Public Gardens Association has more than 600 member gardens and organizations participating in the Go Public Gardens program. Its goal is to help people feel free to get outside and visit a public garden.

The USDA Forest Service is also a partner in the program. With more than 191 million acres of National Forest Lands across the U.S., it’s easy to find a place to spend some time out in the natural environment.

Now’s the perfect time to plan a nature adventure. Have you seen all of the public gardens in your area? Why not visit one during your next vacation? Stop by a garden that’s new to you and your family or revisit a memorable spot that holds a special place in your heart.

Exploring Nature and Enjoying the Holiday

National Public Gardens Day is more than just a one-day celebration. It’s a state of mind and a year-round tribute to nature’s beauty.

Nature walks are good for your mind, body, and soul. They bring benefits, including improved mental health, stress reduction, and stronger social bonds. Public spaces help people relish nature together.

The National Parks Service reports that a 30-minute nature stroll can contribute to lower blood pressure, lower cholesterol, more balanced blood glucose, and better heart health. Over time, you could see benefits like a stronger immune system, lower body-wide inflammation, and reduced anxiety and depression .

Perhaps that’s why forest bathing has become so popular as a form of ecotherapy. When you take a forest bath, you meander slowly through the woods and take time to absorb the natural world around you. Every blossom and frond inspires a newfound sense of inner contentment.

Water Conservation Deserves Celebration

title: Cultivating Community: Exploring the Beauty of Public Gardens (Part 2/2)
content:
that’s why forest bathing has become so popular as a form of ecotherapy. When you take a forest bath, you meander slowly through the woods and take time to absorb the natural world around you. Every blossom and frond inspires a newfound sense of inner contentment. Water Conservation Deserves Celebration

Plants can’t thrive without water and public gardens can’t thrive without water conservation. Rain Bird supports sensible irrigation through The Intelligent Use of Water ™ initiative, which views water as a valuable resource to be used wisely. You’ll see our products in the WaterSense program , which reduces energy and water consumption by 20% or more.

To further advance the mission, Rain Bird provides an array of water-conserving equipment to local public gardens. Innovative equipment helps public gardens save water, save money, and continue providing a public service to the community. Plus, the effort spreads greater awareness that gardens are flourishing with smart watering.

Many of the same watering strategies you’ll see at public gardens can be used right in your own home garden. There’s no need to spend too much time or effort watering your yard.

For example, the Landscape & Garden Drip Watering Kit is a fast and simple way to use drip irrigation to water your plants. It comes with adjustable bubblers and water sprays to provide just the right nourishment for flower beds and decorative shrubs.

High-tech water monitors and automatic timers , like the new ARC and TRU series smart controllers, are managed on your smartphone. This makes it convenient to save water without worrying about timing or weather forecasts. New technology tracks your local forecast and automatically adjusts your irrigation schedule to suit your yard. To learn more about home watering conservation, take a look at our water-saving tips .

Where to Visit Public Lands and Gardens

Some of our favorite public gardens include the Tucson Botanical Gardens and the Desert Botanical Garden in Arizona, which combine desert beauty with excellent water conservation. Another favorite is The Arboretum in California where a 127-acre botanical garden shares space with Rancho Santa Anita’s historic site.

If you can’t make it to a public garden in person this year, embark on the U.S. Botanic Gardens virtual tour . You can also explore a deep woodland in the U.S. Forest Service’s Black Hills Experimental Forest Tour . Virtual tours bring the excitement of nature right into your living room.

How will you celebrate National Public Gardens Day this year? Whether you enjoy it at home or in a public garden, we’ll be right there celebrating with you.

title: TRU Control Over Your Landscape (Part 1/3)
content:
TRU Control Over Your Landscape

If you're a homeowner who dreams of a lush, vibrant yard that is the envy of the neighborhood, then your landscape is more than a hobby; it's a labor of love. But achieving that dream often comes with challenges, especially when it comes to watering. Too little, and your precious plants wither. Too much, and you risk drowning them while wasting a valuable resource. That's where an automatic underground irrigation system comes into play, particularly with the TRU Series irrigation controller from Rain Bird.

How Does an Automatic Irrigation System Work?

You can’t control the rain, but you can control how much water your plants are drinking. An automatic underground irrigation system could be just what your lawn needs to thrive. An automatic underground watering system works by turning valves open or closed using a timer to control your irrigation schedule.

Imagine going out for the day or traveling on vacation. The last thing you want to do is worry about your lawn while you’re out enjoying your trip.

Back home, your watering system is always on alert. With your irrigation controller preprogrammed, your customized schedule clicks on at the right times. Working in the background are your pre-installed sprinklers, bubblers, drip tube, and other watering features to deliver water to your lawn, flower beds, patio container plants , or vegetable garden. From a distance, all you see is a lush, green landscape surrounding your home.

Automatic Watering Systems Support Plant Health

Both underwatering and overwatering can stress your plants. Underwatering causes plants to struggle and become spindly as they wilt in the sun. Overwatering deprives their roots of much-needed oxygen, which can weaken them over time and lead to root rot.

Plants love automated watering systems. They enjoy receiving their favorite drink without puddling, flooding, inconsistent watering, or withering away from too little care. Your new automatic watering system will restore your plants to their full health.

The Rain Bird TRU Series irrigation controller features a rugged design for busy homeowners to manage their landscape watering easily. With 4, 6, or 8 stations and indoor or outdoor options, the TRU Series controller is designed with aesthetics, usability, and reliability in mind. Its robust set screw terminals accommodate AC power and valve connections, and outdoor models come preinstalled in a contractor-grade case to withstand even the harshest environments. The TRU controller is easy to set up, simple to use, and offers a seamless contemporary look. Your plants will get all the attention, not your watering equipment.

See TRU Control in Action

Imagine an intuitive and efficient irrigation system that feels like the heartbeat of your garden, pumping life-giving water through the veins of your landscape with precision and care. This is the promise of the TRU Series irrigation controller.

If you’ve never used an automatic underground watering system before, it may help to see it in action. Take a look at Rain Bird’s video showing how TRU Series Irrigation Controllers work.

Setting It Up, Making It Simple

Designed with the homeowner in mind, the TRU Series boasts an ease of setup and use that demystifies programming your watering schedule. Its sleek, unobtrusive design ensures your plants are the stars of the show, while the intuitive programming dial and multi-function display make managing your garden's hydration a breeze.

Flexibility That Fits Your Life

Life's complicated, but watering your garden doesn't have to be. With the customizable watering options of the TRU Series controller, you can water by days of the week, odd or even calendar days, or cyclic every one to 30 days. The 12 start times across three programs adapt to your schedule, your garden's needs, and the rhythms of nature. Set your TRU system to deliver more frequent watering for grass, flowers, and vegetable gardens, which typically need drinks multiple times per week. We recommend deep watering just once a week for most large trees and well-established perennial shrubs.

Whether adjusting for the seasons, accommodating the unique needs of different plants, or scheduling a day off for a landscaping crew to work in your yard, TRU controllers are as flexible as they are reliable.

Installation and Upkeep Made Easy

After installation, the TRU controller is unobtrusive, but you’ll know it’s handling a big job behind the scenes and have confidence that your landscape will remain healthy and beautiful year-round. Many homeowners enjoy having this small, simple control box that mounts anywhere inside their home or garage. With straightforward maintenance tips , you can ensure your system remains efficient and effective for years.

The TRU Series irrigation controller is an investment in your landscape's health and vibrancy, offering the gift of time and peace of mind. You’ll know that your garden will flourish with Rain Bird's intelligent irrigation solutions.

A Win for You and the Planet

Adopting a TRU Series irrigation controller isn't just a win for your garden; it's a victory for the planet. According to the US Environmental Protection Agency, landscape irrigation accounts for nearly one-third of all residential water use – nearly 9 billion gallons per day .

Rain Bird TRU Controllers help reduce water usage with seasonal adjust technology that changes the watering duration from 5% to 200% for all programs simultaneously or for individual programs associated with run times. There’s even a rain delay feature to save time, money, and water. You can suspend watering for up to 14 days when it rains (applies only to stations set to obey a rain sensor), and your normal watering schedule will automatically resume after the set delay has elapsed.

title: Growing Greener: Water Saving Tips for a Sustainable Lawn (Part 1/2)
content:
Growing Greener: Water Saving Tips for a Sustainable Lawn

As the impacts of climate change intensify, conserving water resources has become a priority across the globe. Fortunately, maintaining a lush, sustainable lawn doesn't have to come at the expense of depleting our water supplies. By embracing smart irrigation practices and leveraging innovative technologies, homeowners can cultivate vibrant outdoor spaces while actively contributing to environmental conservation efforts.

At Rain Bird, we understand the delicate balance between nurturing your thriving landscape and being a responsible steward of our water resources. Our mission is to empower homeowners with the knowledge and tools necessary to achieve this harmony, ensuring that every drop counts towards a greener, more sustainable future.

The Importance of Lawn Irrigation

A well-maintained lawn does more than just make your house look beautiful; it offers many environmental, social, and economic benefits. Your yard acts as a natural air filter, reducing heat and absorbing pollutants while promoting biodiversity by providing habitats for insects, birds, and other wildlife. Moreover, studies have shown that well-kept landscapes can increase property values by up to 20%.

Often overlooked is the vital role lawns play in supporting the health of trees. Without proper irrigation, trees become stressed and susceptible to disease and pests, compromising their ability to produce oxygen, absorb carbon dioxide, and provide shade and shelter for various species.

Tips for Smart Watering

Smart lawn irrigation systems are key to ensuring your landscape receives the right amount of water at the right time while preventing overwatering and promoting water conservation. Rain Bird offers several products to help water your lawn more efficiently. These include:

Pressure regulating stems (PRS) : Reducing water pressure by 10 psi can increase water savings by 5% for sprays and 7% for rotors.

Rotary nozzles : Retrofitting existing spray bodies with rotary nozzles can improve water savings by up to 30% compared to standard spray nozzles.

Drip irrigation : Low-volume drip irrigation systems maintain healthy landscapes with over 90% efficiency by delivering water directly to plant root zones, reducing runoff and evaporation.

Subsurface tubing : Out of sight, out of mind. Dripline installed beneath turf or sod efficiently maintains a healthy lawn while using a fraction of the water.

Smart controllers : High-tech automated irrigation timers, like the new ARC Series with self-adjusting technology reduces water usage by up to 30%.

Here are some tips to help you make the most of your irrigation system:

Water early : Watering your lawn in the early morning when temperatures are lower minimizes water loss to evaporation.

Check the forecast : Adjust your watering schedule based on weather conditions, reducing frequency during rainy periods.

title: Growing Greener: Water Saving Tips for a Sustainable Lawn (Part 2/2)
content:
Here are some tips to help you make the most of your irrigation system: Water early : Watering your lawn in the early morning when temperatures are lower minimizes water loss to evaporation. Check the forecast : Adjust your watering schedule based on weather conditions, reducing frequency during rainy periods.

Implement cycle and soak : Some municipalities mandate limited watering days, promoting deep root growth that helps plants withstand periods without water. However, watering for too long in one period can cause wasteful runoff. Instead, use "cycle and soak" scheduling, which automatically breaks the watering up into multiple “cycles” with a period of “soak” in between, providing sufficient water to plants without causing runoff.

Monitor water usage and check for leaks : If your water bill seems unusually high or has increased without explanation, check your irrigation system for leaks and ensure all equipment is functioning correctly. Don't hesitate to contact a professional irrigation contractor if necessary.

Upgrade to efficient equipment : Installing the latest water-saving irrigation technology can significantly reduce water consumption. Rain Bird's ARC Series Smart Residential Controllers , for instance, can save an average home nearly 7,600 gallons of water annually by using local weather data to automatically adjust watering times depending on the season to prevent under or overwatering, as well as delay programmed schedules in the event of inclement weather.

Proper sprinkler system design offers efficient water coverage tailored to your unique landscape. Rain Bird's Sprinkler Design Service can offer homeowners a comprehensive plan for installation and long-term maintenance, simplifying the entire process of caring for your lawn.

This personalized approach ensures your sprinkler system is optimized for your specific yard, eliminating dry spots and minimizing water waste.

Embracing a Sustainable Future

The importance of water conservation cannot be overstated, and implementing efficient watering systems like automatic lawn irrigation is an obvious choice. By making smart, sustainable choices such as weather-based controllers, pressure regulating stems, rotary nozzles, and drip irrigation, we can drastically reduce water usage while ensuring our landscapes remain verdant and healthy.

Remember, every drop saved today contributes to a more sustainable tomorrow, and together, we can make a lasting impact. title: Living in Harmony: The Benefits of Companion Planting (Part 1/3)
content:
Living in Harmony: The Benefits of Companion Planting

Just like humans, some plants do better when surrounded by others. In fact, many gardeners know that having a diverse mix of plants makes for a healthier garden, but combining certain plants together can also make them more productive. Not only that, but companion planting is also a great way to save space, improve soil health, and even deter unwanted pests.

In this article, we will explore the ins and outs of companion planting, explain how this gardening method can be beneficial, and offer tips on how to start.

What Is Companion Planting?

Companion planting is the practice of growing certain plants together to improve their health and yield. This practice comes from the holistic notion that gardens are like ecosystems where the plants interact and work harmoniously to create a more bio-diverse and healthy environment.

According to research , the science behind this stems from chemical messaging and allelopathy in plants, which essentially refers to a plant’s chemistry affecting the growth of another. In traditional monoculture planting, only planting one type of crop in a single area allows for large-scale farming, but it also increases reliance on fertilizers and pesticides for the plants to survive.

In contrast, polyculture, or companion planting, leads to greater biodiversity because it enhances water conservation and nutrient cycling and helps deter pests and diseases. While this research-based approach was initially designed for larger-scale farming practices, the same idea can be applied to home gardens when planting fruits, veggies, herbs, and flowers.

What Are the Benefits of Companion Planting?

There are several advantages to companion planting in your home garden. While the exact benefits can vary depending on the specific types of plants you choose to grow together, some of the most common are:

Space efficiency

Many homeowners run into issues when wanting to plant a garden, such as not having enough space to plant everything. With companion planting, however, you can maximize space by planting different plants closer together. This is especially ideal for those who are using raised garden beds .

Pest prevention

Planting certain aromatic flowers or herbs next to your fruits and veggies can deter pests. Some companion plants can also act as “trap crops,” which means they draw attention away from your more important crops that you don’t want to ruin.

Improved soil health

Certain companion plants are used because they have deep roots, which help bring nutrients to the soil surface, making those nutrients readily available for other plants whose roots do not run as deep. Another way companion plants help with soil health is by absorbing certain substances, which then changes the chemistry of the soil, making it more beneficial for other plants.

Attracts pollinators

Growing companion flowers in your garden is great for attracting pollinators. Some crops, such as squash, melons, and cucumbers, depend on pollinators to produce their fruit. In general, having a garden that helps support pollinators is important for helping local wildlife and ecosystems survive.

title: Living in Harmony: The Benefits of Companion Planting (Part 2/3)
content:
beneficial for other plants. Attracts pollinators Growing companion flowers in your garden is great for attracting pollinators. Some crops, such as squash, melons, and cucumbers, depend on pollinators to produce their fruit. In general, having a garden that helps support pollinators is important for helping local wildlife and ecosystems survive.

Weed control

Weeds often take control when there is room for them to grow in bare soil. Since companion planting requires planting closer together, it can prevent weeds from taking hold. Buckwheat, for example, is a great weed-controlling companion plant as it only grows in the bare spots of your garden.

Ideal Companion Planting Combinations for Your Garden

To help you get started on your co-planting journey, we’ve put together a little companion plant cheat sheet:

Asparagus, beans, beets, bell peppers, cabbage, marigolds, eggplant, oregano, potatoes, tomatoes

Carrots, chard, beets, peas, corn, cabbage, cucumbers, radishes

Onions, garlic

Cabbage family (kale, cauliflower, broccoli)

Beets, peppermint, sage, onion, chard, corn, spinach, sunflowers

Fennel, pole beans, tomatoes, strawberries

Lettuce, beans, onions, peas, tomatoes, peppers

Sunflowers, corn

Squash, sunflowers, cucumber, marjoram, peas

Beans, radishes, sunflowers, cabbage family, English peas

Aromatic herbs, Irish potatoes

Carrot, beets, lettuce, asparagus, strawberries, tomatoes, cabbage family

Beans, English peas

Basil, beans, garlic, corn, celery, marigolds, onions, horseradish, spinach, peas

Squash, tomatoes, cucumber, raspberries, sunflowers, peppers, melons, cabbage, asparagus

Cabbage, beets, carrots, chives, kale, lettuce, spinach, squash

Strawberries

Corn, beans, dill, marigolds, radishes, sunflowers, strawberries, peas

Broccoli, cabbage

Strawberries

Bush beans, caraway, lettuce, chives, onions, sage, squash, spinach

Cabbage, peppers, tomatoes, potatoes

Parsley, basil, asparagus, carrots, onion, celery, peppers

Corn, potatoes, fennel, cabbage

Corn, beans, dill, garlic, oregano, peas, spinach, radishes, marigolds

Potatoes, pumpkin

Additional Companion Planting Tips

In addition to what plants grow well together, here are a few more tips to help get you started:

Avoid planting in rows: Intermingling herbs and flowers with your veggies and fruits rather than planting in neat rows can help protect against pest attacks.

Try intercropping: Plant your fast-growing edible plants next to slow-growing ones to maximize space. For example, radishes will be ready to harvest when your melons or squash are ready to grow more and take over their spaces.

Match conditions: Grow plants together that prefer similar conditions, such as those that need similar light or water conditions. In the same vein, you should keep plants that are prone to similar diseases apart to reduce the chance of disease spreading.

Shade match: For plants that need more shade, grow them next to taller plants that can block out the sun.

Watering can often be a little tricky when companion planting, so it’s important to try to group plants together with similar water needs. Drip irrigation is a great way to water a companion planted garden . With drip irrigation, you can use drip tubing and various emitters to precisely direct the right amount of water for each plant or area of your garden without overwatering or wasting water.

For more convenience, you can automate your garden irrigation schedule by setting up a watering timer for your hose to ensure your plants get exactly what they need. Rain Bird offers a wide range of drip irrigation products to help get you started.

title: Drip Irrigation vs. Soaker Hoses (Part 1/3)
content:
Efficient Gardening: Why Drip Irrigation Beats Soaker Hoses for Home Landscapes

Water conservation is a critical challenge in residential landscapes. So, choosing a watering system that maximizes every drop is important. With climate patterns shifting and water becoming an even more precious resource, some municipalities mandate limited watering days. At Rain Bird, The Intelligent Use of Water™ is our guiding philosophy that ensures the health and beauty of your garden as well as our global efforts in water conservation. For DIY gardeners and homeowners, finding the right irrigation system can make all the difference in achieving a healthy, water-efficient garden.

Drip irrigation and soaker hoses are two common methods homeowners frequently compare when deciding on an eco-friendly way to water their lawn or garden. In this article, we'll explore the advantages of drip irrigation over soaker hoses.

Understanding Drip Irrigation vs. Soaker Hoses

Before we compare these two systems, we must understand what we're dealing with.

Drip Irrigation is a precise watering method that delivers water directly to the plant's base through a network of tubing, connectors, and emitters. It precisely targets the root zone, minimizing evaporation and runoff.

Soaker Hoses are made from porous materials that allow water to seep out slowly along their length, providing moisture to plants. They can be laid on the ground or buried under mulch to water garden beds, borders, or vegetable patches.

Home gardeners frequently compare drip irrigation and soaker hoses because both involve long tubes that slowly drip water to the base of plants. But the similarities end there. Here are a few reasons drip irrigation shines in its ability to significantly reduce water usage by up to 80% compared to traditional watering methods.

Consistent Watering

A soaker hose is simply a porous tube that releases water along its length. This presents limitations for landscape design and leads to inefficient irrigation. Since soaker hoses are just tubes with holes, water distribution is uneven due to pressure loss, often resulting in overwatering at the start and underwatering at the end, which can harm plant health and waste water.

Drip emitter tubing is an advanced irrigation technology that delivers water directly to the roots of plants through small emitters embedded along the tubing. These pressure compensating emitters ensure that water is delivered consistently from the beginning of the line to the end, even on slight slopes. This precision is essential for maintaining uniform soil moisture levels. It conserves water and promotes robust plant growth by ensuring each plant receives the exact amount of water it needs.

Ease of Installation and Use

Soaker Hoses are commonly installed due to their low cost. Often, they are laid on the ground or under mulch and connected to a water source. However, this low-cost option can lead to frustration as soaker hoses need constant adjustment to achieve even watering.

title: Drip Irrigation vs. Soaker Hoses (Part 2/3)
content:
needs. Ease of Installation and Use Soaker Hoses are commonly installed due to their low cost. Often, they are laid on the ground or under mulch and connected to a water source. However, this low-cost option can lead to frustration as soaker hoses need constant adjustment to achieve even watering.

Installing a drip irrigation system can be just as straightforward as using soaker hoses. Drip irrigation excels in adaptability, offering solutions for various garden layouts , from row crops to raised beds to hanging flower baskets. The wide variety of available components and the ability to customize their placement in your garden ensure that each plant gets its due share of water. You can even automate your drip irrigation system by adding a timer like the Rain Bird Easy to Program Hose End Timer , Electronic Hose End Timer , or Premium Hose End Timer .

Maintenance and Durability

Drip emitter tubing stands out for its resistance to clogging. It’s engineered to reduce the likelihood of dirt and debris build-up, unlike soaker hoses which are more prone to clogging. Additionally, drip emitter tubing is built to withstand UV rays and chemical exposure, making it more durable against environmental factors. Soaker hoses, on the other hand, degrade faster under similar conditions.

Longevity and Environmental Impact

The lifespan of drip emitter tubing is significantly longer than that of soaker hoses. Drip systems are designed for years of use, whereas soaker hoses often require frequent replacements. This durability reduces waste and means a smaller environmental footprint, as it minimizes the need for repeated manufacturing and disposal compared to soaker hoses.

With drip irrigation, every gallon is used productively to water your lawn or garden. Drip irrigation maximizes water usage by delivering water directly to the root zone, reducing runoff and the potential for weed growth and disease.

Subsurface Use and Root Intrusion Prevention

Rain Bird also offers drip emitter tubing that is specifically designed for subsurface use. It can be buried below ground, utilizing a copper shield to prevent root intrusion into the emitters. In contrast, burying soaker hoses often leads to root penetration and leaks, compromising the efficiency and integrity of the irrigation system.

Cost Considerations

While the initial investment in drip emitter tubing may be higher than soaker hoses, the long-term cost savings are substantial. The durability and efficiency of a drip system means fewer replacements and lower water bills over time, making drip a more economical choice in the long run.

Drip irrigation offers several advantages over soaker hoses, including precise water delivery, greater durability, and significant environmental benefits. With drip irrigation, you can create a more efficient and sustainable watering system that keeps your landscape lush, and your garden flourishing without the frustration and wastefulness of soaker hoses.
